stories.title: China unveils 10 incentives for Taiwan after historic Xi-Cheng meeting
stories.description: The package eases tourist curbs, resumes flights, and allows Taiwanese TV dramas on the mainland, capping the first KMT-CCP leadership talks in a decade.

stories.title: US-Iran talks collapse after 21 hours with no deal, Vance says
stories.description: Tehran refused to commit to abandoning nuclear ambitions despite Washington being "quite flexible," the vice president said.

stories.title: Brennan says 25th Amendment 'written with Trump in mind'
stories.description: More than 70 Democratic lawmakers have called for impeachment or the 25th Amendment after Trump threatened to annihilate Iranian civilization.

stories.title: AI data center boom keeps coal plants running, worsening US air quality
stories.description: A Reuters investigation found St. Louis among the hardest-hit cities as regulatory rollbacks and surging power demand from AI undermine decades of clean-air progress.

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV decries 'delusion of omnipotence' fueling Iran war
stories.description: The pope presided over a peace vigil at St. Peter's Basilica as historic U.S.-Iran talks began in Pakistan on Saturday.

stories.title: Trump warns China of 'big problems' over Iran arms
stories.description: The president threatened consequences after reports that Beijing is preparing to send air-defense systems to Tehran in coming weeks.

stories.title: Artemis II crew gets hero's welcome in Houston
stories.description: The four astronauts reunited with their families Saturday after splashing down Friday, capping a record-breaking 10-day voyage around the Moon.

stories.title: Qatar resumes full maritime navigation Sunday amid Gulf ceasefire
stories.description: All vessel types can sail from 6 a.m. to 6 p.m. daily, ending restrictions imposed when the Iran conflict disrupted Gulf shipping in late February.

stories.title: UK shelves Chagos Islands handover after Trump opposition
stories.description: The bill to transfer sovereignty to Mauritius has been pulled from the King's Speech after Washington withdrew its support for the deal.

stories.title: Netanyahu says Israel's Iran campaign 'not over' as U.S.-Iran talks begin in Islamabad
stories.description: The Israeli PM issued a defiant video statement Saturday as Vice President Vance led the first direct U.S.-Iran negotiations since 1979.

stories.title: U.S.-Iran peace talks begin in Pakistan as Wall Street posts best week since November
stories.description: The first direct negotiations between Washington and Tehran in decades got underway Saturday in Islamabad after a ceasefire-fueled rally lifted stocks.

stories.title: Altman ties Molotov cocktail attack to New Yorker expose
stories.description: The OpenAI CEO published a blog post linking the firebombing of his home to rhetoric in the magazine's investigation of his leadership.

stories.title: Lebanon death toll passes 2,000 as Israel-Hezbollah fighting intensifies
stories.description: Israeli forces struck 200 Hezbollah targets in 24 hours as Iran demands a Lebanon ceasefire before U.S. peace talks in Islamabad can proceed.

stories.title: Manhattan DA opens investigation into Swalwell assault allegations
stories.description: The probe follows accusations from a former staffer as top Democrats demand the California governor candidate exit the race.

stories.title: DHS orders furloughed staff back to work despite ongoing shutdown
stories.description: The department recalled all furloughed employees Friday as paychecks begin arriving, but Congress remains on recess with no funding deal.

stories.title: Netanyahu says Iran has no functioning enrichment facilities left
stories.description: The Israeli PM claimed "enormous achievements" in the U.S.-Israeli campaign as peace talks continued in Islamabad on Saturday.

stories.title: GOP lawmakers accuse EU of election meddling in Hungary
stories.description: Reps. Smith and Harris sent a letter to von der Leyen condemning "unprecedented, undemocratic interference" ahead of Sunday's vote.

stories.title: US launches mine clearance mission in Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Two Navy destroyers made the first transit of the strait since the US-Iran conflict began, as CENTCOM began clearing Iranian sea mines.

stories.title: Flávio Bolsonaro edges past Lula for first time in Datafolha poll
stories.description: The Saturday survey shows a statistical tie in a simulated runoff, but marks a symbolic shift as Lula's once-commanding lead evaporates.

stories.title: Retirement speculation intensifies around Justices Alito and Thomas ahead of midterms
stories.description: A Reuters report highlights growing pressure on the Supreme Court's two oldest conservative members as November elections narrow the confirmation window.

stories.title: Iran's new supreme leader has 'severe and disfiguring wounds'
stories.description: The report emerged as U.S. and Iranian delegations held historic face-to-face ceasefire talks in Islamabad, their first direct engagement since 1979.

stories.title: Iran admits it can't find all mines in Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: The disclosure threatens ceasefire terms as VP Vance heads to Islamabad for talks Saturday and tanker traffic remains at a fraction of pre-war levels.

stories.title: Trump administration admits tenfold error in NY Medicaid fraud probe
stories.description: CMS acknowledged Dr. Mehmet Oz overstated the number of New Yorkers receiving personal care services by a factor of 10, citing a billing code misinterpretation.

stories.title: Rubio revokes green cards of Iran hostage crisis spokeswoman's son, family
stories.description: The three Iranian nationals were arrested in Los Angeles and are in ICE custody pending deportation, expanding a crackdown on regime-linked residents.

stories.title: UAE pulls $3.5B from Pakistan over Iran mediation role
stories.description: The withdrawal equals roughly 21% of Pakistan's foreign exchange reserves as Islamabad hosts U.S.-Iran peace talks Saturday.

stories.title: Israel, Lebanon hold first direct call ahead of Tuesday talks in Washington
stories.description: Ambassadors from both countries joined U.S. officials on a preparatory call Friday, with formal negotiations set for April 14 at the State Department.

stories.title: Trump vows to pardon staff before leaving office
stories.description: The president joked about pardoning "everyone who has come within 200 feet of the Oval," according to people familiar with his remarks.

stories.title: Iran claims US agreed to unfreeze $6B; White House denies
stories.description: Dueling claims over frozen Iranian funds emerge as U.S.-Iran peace talks get underway in Islamabad under an April 22 ceasefire deadline.

stories.title: Harris says she's 'thinking about' running for president in 2028
stories.description: The former vice president made the comments at Rev. Al Sharpton's National Action Network convention in New York City on Thursday.

stories.title: Trump unveils design for gilded 250-foot arch in DC
stories.description: The proposed Independence Arch would feature a winged figure, golden eagles and lions, and become the world's largest monumental arch.

stories.title: Ukraine nearing peace deal with Russia, top aide says
stories.description: Zelenskyy's chief of staff Kyrylo Budanov told Bloomberg he sees progress in negotiations, saying a resolution "will not take long."

stories.title: Massachusetts high court rules Meta must face youth addiction suit
stories.description: The first state supreme court to weigh Section 230's reach found the law does not shield Meta from claims its platform design harmed minors.

stories.title: Trump's Board of Peace receives fraction of $17B pledged for Gaza
stories.description: Only 3 of 10 countries that pledged funds have contributed, with the Iran war compounding donor reluctance, sources told Reuters.

stories.title: Hezbollah vows to fight 'until last breath' ahead of Israel-Lebanon talks
stories.description: Secretary-General Naim Qassem warned Lebanese officials against "free concessions" as Washington prepares to host direct negotiations next week.

stories.title: Consumer sentiment hits all-time low as April reading falls over 10%
stories.description: The University of Michigan's preliminary April index broke the June 2022 record, driven by surging gas prices and the Iran conflict's economic fallout.

stories.title: Macron invites Trump to Versailles dinner after G7 summit
stories.description: The French president is trying to lure Trump to the June gathering with an exclusive bilateral event at the iconic palace, sources say.

stories.title: EU and US near critical minerals deal to counter China
stories.description: A draft action plan would create price floors and other incentives to advantage non-Chinese suppliers across the entire minerals value chain.

stories.title: Zelenskyy confirms Ukraine shot down Iranian drones in Middle East
stories.description: Ukrainian forces actively intercepted Shahed drones in multiple countries, Zelenskyy revealed, marking Kyiv's first acknowledged combat role in the region.

stories.title: UK and France deepen defense ties amid rift with Trump
stories.description: Britain and France signed a joint missile development pact and boosted military spending as both nations push for European security independence from the U.S.

stories.title: Oil spill shuts down Port of Antwerp, Europe's No. 2 cargo hub
stories.description: A bunkering spill spread overnight into the River Scheldt, closing critical locks and leaving dozens of vessels stranded at the Belgian port.

stories.title: CPI jumps to 3.3% as energy prices surge amid Iran conflict
stories.description: March consumer prices rose 0.9% in a single month, the sharpest increase since 2005, driven by a 21.2% spike in gasoline prices tied to the Strait of Hormuz disruption.

stories.title: Dubai caps foreign airlines at one daily flight until May 31
stories.description: Indian carriers face the steepest losses as wartime restrictions limit each foreign airline to a single round trip per day at the world's busiest international hub.

stories.title: IDF chief says Israel is 'in a state of war' in Lebanon, not a ceasefire
stories.description: Hezbollah rockets caused power outages in northern Israel on Friday as Lt. Gen. Zamir declared Lebanon the IDF's "primary combat zone."

stories.title: TotalEnergies shuts SATORP refinery after Iran strike damages processing train
stories.description: Saudi Arabia says Iranian attacks have cut over 1.3 million barrels per day of oil capacity and killed three people since the war began.

stories.title: First-ever egg from a mammal ancestor found in 250M-year-old fossil
stories.description: Scientists confirmed that mammal ancestors laid soft-shelled eggs, resolving a question that has stumped evolutionary biologists for nearly two centuries.

stories.title: Bessent, Powell summon bank CEOs over Anthropic AI cyber risks
stories.description: Treasury Secretary Bessent and Fed Chair Powell warned Wall Street leaders at an urgent Tuesday meeting about threats from Anthropic's new model.

stories.title: Finland begins trial run at world's first permanent nuclear waste site
stories.description: Posiva announced it has started placing test canisters at Onkalo, the underground repository built to entomb spent fuel for millennia.

stories.title: Bank of Korea holds rates as BOJ flags stagflation risk
stories.description: South Korea kept its benchmark rate at 2.50% for a seventh straight meeting while Japan's wholesale inflation accelerated amid Middle East energy shock.

stories.title: TSMC Q1 revenue jumps 35%, beating forecasts on AI demand
stories.description: The world's largest contract chipmaker posted $35.7 billion in quarterly sales, topping estimates as AI chip orders held firm despite Middle East tensions.

stories.title: Xi Jinping meets Taiwan's KMT leader in Beijing
stories.description: Cheng Li-wun's visit to the Great Hall of the People marks the first meeting between a sitting KMT chair and China's leader in a decade.

stories.title: Lawsuit filed after USC freshman loses eye at protest
stories.description: An attorney filed a federal tort claim against DHS on behalf of Tucker Collins, 18, who was blinded by a projectile at a March 28 "No Kings" rally in L.A.

stories.title: New charter lets Kennedy reshape CDC vaccine panel after court loss
stories.description: The revised charter broadens who can serve on the advisory committee and shifts its focus toward vaccine injury claims long promoted by skeptics.

stories.title: Judge finds Pentagon violating order on press access
stories.description: U.S. District Judge Paul Friedman ruled Thursday that the Defense Department defied his earlier order by imposing new restrictions that still block meaningful reporter access.

stories.title: OpenAI pitches compute edge over Anthropic as revenue gap widens
stories.description: Bloomberg reports OpenAI told investors this week that its infrastructure spending gives it a key advantage over Anthropic, which recently surpassed OpenAI's revenue run rate.

stories.title: White House warns staff against prediction market bets amid Iran trading scrutiny
stories.description: The directive followed suspicious trading surges that repeatedly preceded Trump's announcements on the Iran conflict, triggering bipartisan legislative action.

stories.title: Cuba's Diaz-Canel vows not to resign in historic NBC interview
stories.description: The Cuban president told "Meet the Press" that "resigning is not part of our vocabulary," becoming the first Cuban leader on U.S. TV in decades.

stories.title: House GOP blocks Democrats' Iran war powers vote during recess
stories.description: Rep. Chris Smith gaveled out of a pro forma session Thursday before Democrats could seek unanimous consent on a resolution to end the Iran conflict.

stories.title: White House confirms Cuba talks continuing 'at the highest level'
stories.description: Press secretary Karoline Leavitt said discussions are ongoing but announced no policy changes, as Díaz-Canel calls talks "difficult."

stories.title: Small AI startups win Pentagon contracts after Anthropic split
stories.description: Defense-focused firms like Smack Technologies and EdgeRunner AI report surging military interest after the Pentagon designated Anthropic a supply-chain risk.

stories.title: US fertility rate fell to record low in 2025, CDC reports
stories.description: The lifetime fertility rate dropped to 1.57 births per woman, well below the 2.1 replacement level, despite pro-natalist policy efforts.

stories.title: Trump says he's 'very optimistic' on Iran deal amid Lebanon crisis
stories.description: VP Vance is set to lead U.S. talks with Iran in Islamabad on Saturday as Israeli strikes on Lebanon strain a days-old ceasefire.

stories.title: Software stocks slide as Anthropic's Mythos AI model stokes disruption fears
stories.description: Palantir fell sharply and the broader software sector declined after the company withheld wide release of a model that found thousands of zero-day flaws.

stories.title: DOJ opens antitrust investigation into NFL over streaming practices over streaming push
stories.description: The probe examines whether the league's shift of games to streaming platforms harms consumers and violates its broadcast exemption.

stories.title: PCE inflation holds at 2.8% in February as jobless claims jump
stories.description: The Fed's preferred inflation gauge stayed unchanged in February while weekly unemployment filings rose more than expected, signaling a mixed economic outlook.

stories.title: Earth grew 16% brighter at night since 2014, study finds
stories.description: Daily satellite imagery reveals the planet's artificial lighting is far more volatile than scientists assumed, with regional brightening and dimming driven by development, conflict, and policy.

stories.title: OpenAI pauses Stargate UK data center over energy costs
stories.description: The decision to shelve the sovereign AI infrastructure project is a setback for Prime Minister Starmer's national AI strategy.

stories.title: Lobbyists with Trump Jr. ties helped Chinese firm win national security review
stories.description: A firm run by Donald Trump Jr.'s hunting companion helped Grand Pharmaceutical defeat a U.S. national security review of its investment in a medical-device startup.

stories.title: Iran war doubles Russia's oil tax revenue to $9 bn in April
stories.description: Reuters calculations show the mineral extraction tax windfall follows months of declining energy revenues and a record budget deficit.

stories.title: UK forces Russian submarines to retreat from undersea cables
stories.description: Britain and Norway tracked three Russian submarines near critical underwater infrastructure for over a month, forcing all vessels to withdraw.

stories.title: IUCN lists emperor penguins as endangered over sea ice loss
stories.description: The Antarctic fur seal and southern elephant seal were also reclassified as climate change reshapes polar ecosystems.

stories.title: New model suggests dark matter has two particle states
stories.description: Fermilab physicists propose that dark matter consists of two distinct particles, potentially explaining a longstanding gamma-ray puzzle.

stories.title: Study: 2.5 million Americans lost food aid after GOP megabill
stories.description: The Center on Budget and Policy Priorities found SNAP enrollment fell sharply in the six months after the One Big Beautiful Bill Act took effect.

stories.title: ArcelorMittal donated foreign steel for Trump's ballroom, then got tariff relief
stories.description: The Luxembourg-based steelmaker supplied $37 million in steel for the White House project while the administration cut tariffs on its Canadian exports.

stories.title: UMass physicists link record-energy neutrino to exploding black hole
stories.description: A "dark charge" model proposes primordial black holes from the early universe could explain a 2023 particle detection that defied all known physics.

stories.title: North Korea tests cluster warhead on ballistic missile in military trial
stories.description: Pyongyang conducted three days of weapons trials, including an electromagnetic weapon system and carbon fiber decoy bombs, drawing condemnation from Seoul.

stories.title: Trump asks New York's top court to toss civil fraud finding
stories.description: The filing cites former attorney Michael Cohen's claims that he was "pressured and coerced" by prosecutors into testifying against Trump.

stories.title: Former Army employee indicted for leaking military secrets to journalist
stories.description: Courtney Williams, 40, allegedly shared classified information used in the bestselling book "The Fort Bragg Cartel."

stories.title: Anti-war protests sweep US cities despite Iran ceasefire
stories.description: Demonstrators in dozens of cities demand a permanent end to the conflict, calling the two-week truce insufficient.

stories.title: Trump eyes Fed adviser Christopher Phelan as chief economist
stories.description: The Minneapolis Fed adviser and University of Minnesota professor would fill a post vacant since February amid rising gas prices.

stories.title: Appeals court upholds Pentagon blacklisting of Anthropic
stories.description: The D.C. Circuit ruled the military's need to secure AI technology during conflict outweighs financial harm to the company.

stories.title: Russian warship escorts sanctioned tankers through English Channel
stories.description: The frigate Admiral Grigorovich sailed alongside two oil tankers off England's south coast, defying the UK's threat to seize shadow fleet vessels.

stories.title: Minnesota districts ask judge to block ICE enforcement near schools
stories.description: Two school districts and the state's teachers union urged a federal judge Wednesday to restore restrictions rescinded by the Trump administration.

stories.title: 'Ketamine Queen' sentenced to 15 years in Matthew Perry's death
stories.description: Jasveen Sangha, who sold the fatal dose of ketamine to the "Friends" star, apologized for her "hand in this tragic outcome" before sentencing.

stories.title: UAE demands Iran pay reparations after 40 days of attacks
stories.description: Abu Dhabi seeks clarity on the U.S.-Iran ceasefire terms while declaring victory after intercepting thousands of missiles and drones.

stories.title: Attorney says man shot by ICE was acquitted, had no warrant
stories.description: The lawyer for Carlos Ivan Mendoza Hernandez presented Salvadoran court documents contradicting DHS claims that his client was a wanted gang member.

stories.title: Iran’s parliament speaker says cease-fire terms already violated
stories.description: Iran's parliament speaker says three clauses of Tehran's 10-point framework have been breached, as Israeli strikes kill at least 254 in Lebanon.

stories.title: Fed minutes reveal growing caution over Iran war, inflation
stories.description: Wednesday's release showed policymakers increasingly wary of cutting rates as oil prices and geopolitical risks cloud the economic outlook.

stories.title: Zeldin tells climate skeptics to 'celebrate' endangerment finding repeal
stories.description: The EPA chief defended his agency's rollback of the foundational climate rule at a Heartland Institute conference in Washington on Wednesday.

stories.title: Trump weighs relocating troops to punish NATO allies over Iran war
stories.description: The plan would move U.S. forces away from countries deemed uncooperative during the conflict and into more supportive member states.

stories.title: Sharif's 'draft' post gaffe raises questions about Pakistan's mediator role
stories.description: PM Shehbaz Sharif accidentally posted a "Draft" label on his ceasefire appeal, fueling speculation the message was scripted by Washington.

stories.title: Iran faces economic collapse after 40 days of war
stories.description: A two-week ceasefire halted U.S.-Israeli bombardment, but Reuters reports Iran is "near the brink of economic collapse" with GDP expected to contract sharply.

stories.title: Pentagon says US ready to resume Iran combat if ceasefire falters
stories.description: Defense Secretary Hegseth and Gen. Caine declared military victory but warned forces will "restart at a moment's notice" as Iranian strikes test the truce.

stories.title: Meta unveils Muse Spark, its first AI model from superintelligence team
stories.description: The closed-source model now powers Meta AI in the U.S., with plans to expand across Instagram, WhatsApp, Facebook, and smart glasses.

stories.title: Jeff Shell fired as Paramount president after leak allegations
stories.description: Shell's ouster follows a $150 million lawsuit accusing him of leaking confidential deal information to a high-stakes gambler.

stories.title: Israel learned of Iran cease-fire deal at last minute
stories.description: Netanyahu was informed by Trump only moments before the two-week truce was announced, and Israel was not part of the negotiations, according to the Journal.

stories.title: DOJ says fired AG Bondi won't appear for Epstein deposition
stories.description: The department argues her subpoena was tied to a role she no longer holds, but the Oversight Committee says it will pursue her testimony.

stories.title: IRGC takes de facto control of Iran as supreme leader reportedly incapacitated
stories.description: Multiple intelligence sources indicate Mojtaba Khamenei is unconscious in Qom, unable to govern, as a military council runs the country.

stories.title: Russia pushes three lunar missions to 2032-2036
stories.description: The delays were announced the same day NASA's Artemis II crew completed a historic moon flyby, the first crewed lunar flight since 1972.

stories.title: NYT names Adam Back as likely Bitcoin creator Satoshi Nakamoto
stories.description: A yearlong investigation built a circumstantial case using writing analysis and timeline gaps, but Back denied being Satoshi more than six times.

stories.title: Hegseth declares US victory as Iran ceasefire takes effect
stories.description: Trump agreed to a two-week ceasefire Tuesday night after a 38-day air campaign, contingent on Iran reopening the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: SK Hynix jumps 15% as Samsung's record forecast lifts chip stocks
stories.description: Samsung's preliminary Q1 operating profit of 57.2 trillion won, an eightfold annual increase, fueled a broader rally in Korean chipmakers.

stories.title: IATA chief warns jet fuel recovery will take months despite ceasefire
stories.description: Willie Walsh said Strait of Hormuz disruptions damaged Middle East refining capacity, meaning supply cannot quickly rebound even after a U.S.-Iran ceasefire.

stories.title: Iran ceasefire reinforces view US emerged weakened, Bloomberg reports
stories.description: Allies and adversaries see the 40-day campaign as a strategic setback that bolstered China and Russia while draining American military and diplomatic capital.

stories.title: U.S. rebuffs Ford's request for aluminum tariff relief
stories.description: The denial comes as fires at a key Novelis plant have disrupted 40% of U.S. auto aluminum supply since September 2025, according to The Wall Street Journal.

stories.title: Amsterdam researchers build metamaterials that learn to change shape
stories.description: Worm-like synthetic materials can learn, forget, and relearn new shapes without a central processor, mimicking living systems.

stories.title: China helped push Iran to accept ceasefire, AP reports
stories.description: Beijing urged Tehran to agree to the two-week truce brokered by Pakistan, as peace talks are set to begin Friday in Islamabad.

stories.title: Judge preserves abortion pill mail access, warns FDA review can't wait
stories.description: A Louisiana federal judge declined to block mifepristone prescriptions by mail but signaled the state could eventually prevail.

stories.title: Fuller wins Georgia runoff to replace Greene by narrow margin
stories.description: Trump-backed Republican Clay Fuller defeated Democrat Shawn Harris by roughly 4 points in one of the most conservative districts in the country.

stories.title: US, Israel strike Iran's largest aluminum plant
stories.description: Strikes on IRALCO in Arak sparked a major fire as Trump warned "a whole civilization will die tonight" if Iran doesn't reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Appeals panel signals 'Alligator Alcatraz' can stay open
stories.description: Judges on the 11th Circuit questioned whether the Florida ICE facility qualifies as a federal action requiring environmental review.

stories.title: CIA used secret 'Ghost Murmur' tool to find airman in Iran
stories.description: The never-before-deployed technology uses quantum magnetometry to detect a human heartbeat, pinpointing the injured crew member hiding in a mountain crevice.

stories.title: Kuwait orders residents indoors as Trump's Iran deadline looms
stories.description: Kuwait's Interior Ministry urged citizens and residents to stay home overnight as Trump's 8 p.m. ET deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz approaches.

stories.title: Nearly 1,000 migrants died in Mediterranean in 2026 so far, IOM says
stories.description: The UN migration agency called it one of the deadliest starts to a year since it began tracking data in 2014, with deaths up over 150% in the central Mediterranean.

stories.title: ICE arrested 800+ people using TSA traveler data
stories.description: Internal ICE records reviewed by Reuters reveal the scope of airport-based immigration enforcement far exceeds prior public estimates.

stories.title: Acting AG Blanche says Trump has 'right' to influence DOJ probes
stories.description: In his first press conference, Blanche also announced a new National Fraud Enforcement Division and denied targeting Trump's political enemies.

stories.title: Vance says he's 'here to help' Orban win Hungary election
stories.description: The vice president accused the EU and Ukrainian intelligence of meddling in Hungary's April 12 vote, offering no evidence for either claim.

stories.title: UK bars Ye from entering country, Wireless Festival canceled
stories.description: The Home Office denied the rapper's travel authorization over antisemitism concerns, and organizers scrapped the three-day London event entirely.

stories.title: UK, Canada push back on Trump's Iran threats as 8 p.m. deadline nears
stories.description: Downing Street signaled it won't allow U.S. use of British bases for infrastructure strikes, while Carney urged respect for international law.

stories.title: Turkish drillship arrives in Somali waters for historic first offshore oil drilling
stories.description: The Çağrı Bey will target the Curad-1 well, roughly 370 km off the coast, in a region estimated to hold up to 40 billion barrels of oil and gas.

stories.title: Tehran's Rafi-Nia Synagogue destroyed in US-Israeli strikes
stories.description: Iran's Jewish community condemned the destruction after overnight airstrikes leveled the historic synagogue, burying Torah scrolls under rubble.

stories.title: US strikes Kharg Island again as war with Iran intensifies
stories.description: Trump warned "a whole civilization will die tonight" if Iran doesn't agree to a deal, as airstrikes pounded Tehran and missiles hit Saudi Arabia.

stories.title: Wall Street firm sends analyst into Strait of Hormuz by speedboat
stories.description: Citrini Research dispatched a field analyst with $15,000 in cash and hidden recording gear to observe Iranian shipping controls firsthand.

stories.title: IEA chief says Middle East war will accelerate renewables shift
stories.description: Fatih Birol told Le Figaro the energy crisis is worse than those of 1973, 1979 and 2022 combined but will transform global energy geopolitics.

stories.title: Trump lawyers invoke immunity ruling to claim sweeping executive power
stories.description: An ABC News review found the administration cited the 2024 decision at least 21 times in emergency appeals to expand presidential authority.

stories.title: Leaked transcript shows Orbán offered to serve Putin
stories.description: Bloomberg published a Hungarian government transcript of an October call in which Orbán told Putin, "I am at your service."

stories.title: Iran war energy shock accelerates global return to nuclear power
stories.description: Countries across Asia are racing to restart reactors and build new plants as the Middle East conflict disrupts roughly one-fifth of global LNG supply.

stories.title: Deere agrees to $99M settlement and digital tools in right-to-repair suit
stories.description: The proposed deal also commits the company to providing farmers digital repair tools for a decade. A separate FTC lawsuit remains active.

stories.title: SpaceX details record IPO plans, eyes June roadshow
stories.description: CFO Bret Johnsen told bankers Monday that retail investors will get "a bigger part than any IPO in history," with a June 11 event for 1,500 individual investors.

stories.title: Federal judge upholds Dugan's ICE obstruction conviction
stories.description: U.S. District Judge Lynn Adelman rejected the former Milwaukee County judge's bid for acquittal or a new trial, clearing the way for sentencing and a 7th Circuit appeal.

stories.title: Artemis II crew witnesses solar eclipse after shattering distance record
stories.description: The four astronauts saw a 53-minute total solar eclipse from beyond the Moon, capping a day of record-breaking firsts during humanity's return to lunar orbit.

stories.title: Iran-launched drone kills couple in Erbil village
stories.description: The Peshmerga ministry warned it may abandon restraint if Baghdad fails to act against militias behind the escalating aerial campaign.

stories.title: Iran launches fresh missile barrage at southern Israel
stories.description: Coordinated attacks from Iran, Yemen, and Hezbollah mark a new multi-front escalation as the war enters its sixth week.

stories.title: Israel orders evacuation of 41 towns in southern Lebanon
stories.description: The Monday evening directive expands the buffer zone to the Zahrani River as airstrikes intensify across Lebanon, killing dozens over the weekend.

stories.title: Iranian drone strike wounds 15 US troops at Kuwait air base
stories.description: CBS News reports most of the injured service members at Ali Al Salem Air Base have returned to duty, as the US casualty toll in the Iran war rises to 373 wounded.

stories.title: USS Murtha departs San Diego to recover Artemis II crew
stories.description: The Navy recovery ship set sail Monday as the four astronauts performed their lunar flyby, with splashdown set for Friday.

stories.title: Saudi Arabia intercepts missiles as debris falls near energy sites
stories.description: Up to 11 ballistic missiles were fired at the kingdom's oil-rich Eastern Province, with damage assessment ongoing at undisclosed energy facilities.

stories.title: Four IDF combat soldiers arrested for suspected Iran espionage
stories.description: The arrests mark one of the most serious spy cases since the war began, with two other Iran-linked espionage affairs revealed the same day.

stories.title: Minneapolis releases video contradicting ICE's account of January shooting
stories.description: Footage shows a 12-second scuffle, not the three-minute assault with a shovel and broom that officers described under oath.

stories.title: Rep. Ansari moves to impeach Hegseth over Iran war
stories.description: The Arizona Democrat, the only Iranian American in Congress, accused the defense secretary of war crimes and reckless endangerment of U.S. troops.

stories.title: UN issues urgent call for aid as Cuba crisis worsens
stories.description: Blackouts exceeding 18 hours a day affect millions as the UN unveils an updated plan to support two million people across eight provinces.

stories.title: Israeli strikes kill at least 10 near Gaza school
stories.description: A WHO driver was also killed in a separate attack, prompting the suspension of all medical evacuations from Gaza via Rafah.

stories.title: Lutnick set for May 6 House interview on Epstein ties
stories.description: The commerce secretary's voluntary transcribed interview before the Oversight Committee follows months of bipartisan pressure over his connections to the late sex offender.

stories.title: OpenAI urges state AGs to probe Musk ahead of April trial
stories.description: The company accused Musk of waging a destabilization campaign, including allegedly coordinating with Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg.

stories.title: Education Dept. terminates agreements protecting trans students at six schools
stories.description: The department ended Title IX settlements with five school districts and a college that required protections for transgender students from discrimination.

stories.title: White House proposes cutting 9,400 TSA jobs, $1.5B from budget
stories.description: The plan would shrink the post-9/11 agency's workforce by roughly 16% and reduce its $7.8 billion budget by about 20%, according to budget documents.

stories.title: US-Israeli strikes destroy AI data center at Tehran university
stories.description: The predawn attack reduced Sharif University of Technology's ICT building to rubble, also damaging labs, a mosque, and faculty buildings.

stories.title: Appeals court bars New Jersey from regulating Kalshi
stories.description: The Third Circuit ruled 2-1 that federal commodities law preempts state gambling enforcement, marking Kalshi's first appellate victory.

stories.title: Trump threatens to jail journalist over Iran F-15 rescue leak
stories.description: The president claims media reporting on the downed jet tipped off Iran and endangered the wounded airman's rescue behind enemy lines.

stories.title: Study of 1,700 languages finds shared grammar rules
stories.description: About one-third of long-proposed "linguistic universals" hold up under modern statistical testing, pointing to deep cognitive forces shaping all human languages.

stories.title: Supreme Court vacates Bannon's contempt-of-Congress conviction
stories.description: The justices returned the case to a lower court where prosecutors have already moved to dismiss the indictment tied to Bannon's Jan. 6 subpoena defiance.

top.title: China unveils 10 incentives for Taiwan after historic Xi-Cheng meeting
top.description: The package eases tourist curbs, resumes flights, and allows Taiwanese TV dramas on the mainland, capping the first KMT-CCP leadership talks in a decade.

top.title: US-Iran talks collapse after 21 hours with no deal, Vance says
top.description: Tehran refused to commit to abandoning nuclear ambitions despite Washington being "quite flexible," the vice president said.

top.title: Brennan says 25th Amendment 'written with Trump in mind'
top.description: More than 70 Democratic lawmakers have called for impeachment or the 25th Amendment after Trump threatened to annihilate Iranian civilization.

top.title: AI data center boom keeps coal plants running, worsening US air quality
top.description: A Reuters investigation found St. Louis among the hardest-hit cities as regulatory rollbacks and surging power demand from AI undermine decades of clean-air progress.

top.title: Pope Leo XIV decries 'delusion of omnipotence' fueling Iran war
top.description: The pope presided over a peace vigil at St. Peter's Basilica as historic U.S.-Iran talks began in Pakistan on Saturday.

top.title: Trump warns China of 'big problems' over Iran arms
top.description: The president threatened consequences after reports that Beijing is preparing to send air-defense systems to Tehran in coming weeks.

top.title: Artemis II crew gets hero's welcome in Houston
top.description: The four astronauts reunited with their families Saturday after splashing down Friday, capping a record-breaking 10-day voyage around the Moon.

top.title: Qatar resumes full maritime navigation Sunday amid Gulf ceasefire
top.description: All vessel types can sail from 6 a.m. to 6 p.m. daily, ending restrictions imposed when the Iran conflict disrupted Gulf shipping in late February.

top.title: UK shelves Chagos Islands handover after Trump opposition
top.description: The bill to transfer sovereignty to Mauritius has been pulled from the King's Speech after Washington withdrew its support for the deal.

top.title: Netanyahu says Israel's Iran campaign 'not over' as U.S.-Iran talks begin in Islamabad
top.description: The Israeli PM issued a defiant video statement Saturday as Vice President Vance led the first direct U.S.-Iran negotiations since 1979.

top.title: U.S.-Iran peace talks begin in Pakistan as Wall Street posts best week since November
top.description: The first direct negotiations between Washington and Tehran in decades got underway Saturday in Islamabad after a ceasefire-fueled rally lifted stocks.

top.title: Altman ties Molotov cocktail attack to New Yorker expose
top.description: The OpenAI CEO published a blog post linking the firebombing of his home to rhetoric in the magazine's investigation of his leadership.

top.title: Lebanon death toll passes 2,000 as Israel-Hezbollah fighting intensifies
top.description: Israeli forces struck 200 Hezbollah targets in 24 hours as Iran demands a Lebanon ceasefire before U.S. peace talks in Islamabad can proceed.

top.title: Manhattan DA opens investigation into Swalwell assault allegations
top.description: The probe follows accusations from a former staffer as top Democrats demand the California governor candidate exit the race.

top.title: DHS orders furloughed staff back to work despite ongoing shutdown
top.description: The department recalled all furloughed employees Friday as paychecks begin arriving, but Congress remains on recess with no funding deal.

top.title: Netanyahu says Iran has no functioning enrichment facilities left
top.description: The Israeli PM claimed "enormous achievements" in the U.S.-Israeli campaign as peace talks continued in Islamabad on Saturday.

top.title: GOP lawmakers accuse EU of election meddling in Hungary
top.description: Reps. Smith and Harris sent a letter to von der Leyen condemning "unprecedented, undemocratic interference" ahead of Sunday's vote.

top.title: US launches mine clearance mission in Strait of Hormuz
top.description: Two Navy destroyers made the first transit of the strait since the US-Iran conflict began, as CENTCOM began clearing Iranian sea mines.

top.title: Flávio Bolsonaro edges past Lula for first time in Datafolha poll
top.description: The Saturday survey shows a statistical tie in a simulated runoff, but marks a symbolic shift as Lula's once-commanding lead evaporates.

top.title: Retirement speculation intensifies around Justices Alito and Thomas ahead of midterms
top.description: A Reuters report highlights growing pressure on the Supreme Court's two oldest conservative members as November elections narrow the confirmation window.

top.title: Iran's new supreme leader has 'severe and disfiguring wounds'
top.description: The report emerged as U.S. and Iranian delegations held historic face-to-face ceasefire talks in Islamabad, their first direct engagement since 1979.

top.title: Iran admits it can't find all mines in Strait of Hormuz
top.description: The disclosure threatens ceasefire terms as VP Vance heads to Islamabad for talks Saturday and tanker traffic remains at a fraction of pre-war levels.

top.title: Trump administration admits tenfold error in NY Medicaid fraud probe
top.description: CMS acknowledged Dr. Mehmet Oz overstated the number of New Yorkers receiving personal care services by a factor of 10, citing a billing code misinterpretation.

top.title: Rubio revokes green cards of Iran hostage crisis spokeswoman's son, family
top.description: The three Iranian nationals were arrested in Los Angeles and are in ICE custody pending deportation, expanding a crackdown on regime-linked residents.

top.title: UAE pulls $3.5B from Pakistan over Iran mediation role
top.description: The withdrawal equals roughly 21% of Pakistan's foreign exchange reserves as Islamabad hosts U.S.-Iran peace talks Saturday.

top.title: Israel, Lebanon hold first direct call ahead of Tuesday talks in Washington
top.description: Ambassadors from both countries joined U.S. officials on a preparatory call Friday, with formal negotiations set for April 14 at the State Department.

top.title: Trump vows to pardon staff before leaving office
top.description: The president joked about pardoning "everyone who has come within 200 feet of the Oval," according to people familiar with his remarks.

top.title: Iran claims US agreed to unfreeze $6B; White House denies
top.description: Dueling claims over frozen Iranian funds emerge as U.S.-Iran peace talks get underway in Islamabad under an April 22 ceasefire deadline.

top.title: Harris says she's 'thinking about' running for president in 2028
top.description: The former vice president made the comments at Rev. Al Sharpton's National Action Network convention in New York City on Thursday.

top.title: Trump unveils design for gilded 250-foot arch in DC
top.description: The proposed Independence Arch would feature a winged figure, golden eagles and lions, and become the world's largest monumental arch.

top.title: Ukraine nearing peace deal with Russia, top aide says
top.description: Zelenskyy's chief of staff Kyrylo Budanov told Bloomberg he sees progress in negotiations, saying a resolution "will not take long."

top.title: Massachusetts high court rules Meta must face youth addiction suit
top.description: The first state supreme court to weigh Section 230's reach found the law does not shield Meta from claims its platform design harmed minors.

top.title: Trump's Board of Peace receives fraction of $17B pledged for Gaza
top.description: Only 3 of 10 countries that pledged funds have contributed, with the Iran war compounding donor reluctance, sources told Reuters.

top.title: Hezbollah vows to fight 'until last breath' ahead of Israel-Lebanon talks
top.description: Secretary-General Naim Qassem warned Lebanese officials against "free concessions" as Washington prepares to host direct negotiations next week.

top.title: Consumer sentiment hits all-time low as April reading falls over 10%
top.description: The University of Michigan's preliminary April index broke the June 2022 record, driven by surging gas prices and the Iran conflict's economic fallout.

top.title: Macron invites Trump to Versailles dinner after G7 summit
top.description: The French president is trying to lure Trump to the June gathering with an exclusive bilateral event at the iconic palace, sources say.

top.title: EU and US near critical minerals deal to counter China
top.description: A draft action plan would create price floors and other incentives to advantage non-Chinese suppliers across the entire minerals value chain.

top.title: Zelenskyy confirms Ukraine shot down Iranian drones in Middle East
top.description: Ukrainian forces actively intercepted Shahed drones in multiple countries, Zelenskyy revealed, marking Kyiv's first acknowledged combat role in the region.

top.title: UK and France deepen defense ties amid rift with Trump
top.description: Britain and France signed a joint missile development pact and boosted military spending as both nations push for European security independence from the U.S.

top.title: Oil spill shuts down Port of Antwerp, Europe's No. 2 cargo hub
top.description: A bunkering spill spread overnight into the River Scheldt, closing critical locks and leaving dozens of vessels stranded at the Belgian port.

top.title: CPI jumps to 3.3% as energy prices surge amid Iran conflict
top.description: March consumer prices rose 0.9% in a single month, the sharpest increase since 2005, driven by a 21.2% spike in gasoline prices tied to the Strait of Hormuz disruption.

top.title: Dubai caps foreign airlines at one daily flight until May 31
top.description: Indian carriers face the steepest losses as wartime restrictions limit each foreign airline to a single round trip per day at the world's busiest international hub.

top.title: IDF chief says Israel is 'in a state of war' in Lebanon, not a ceasefire
top.description: Hezbollah rockets caused power outages in northern Israel on Friday as Lt. Gen. Zamir declared Lebanon the IDF's "primary combat zone."

top.title: TotalEnergies shuts SATORP refinery after Iran strike damages processing train
top.description: Saudi Arabia says Iranian attacks have cut over 1.3 million barrels per day of oil capacity and killed three people since the war began.

top.title: First-ever egg from a mammal ancestor found in 250M-year-old fossil
top.description: Scientists confirmed that mammal ancestors laid soft-shelled eggs, resolving a question that has stumped evolutionary biologists for nearly two centuries.

top.title: Bessent, Powell summon bank CEOs over Anthropic AI cyber risks
top.description: Treasury Secretary Bessent and Fed Chair Powell warned Wall Street leaders at an urgent Tuesday meeting about threats from Anthropic's new model.

top.title: Finland begins trial run at world's first permanent nuclear waste site
top.description: Posiva announced it has started placing test canisters at Onkalo, the underground repository built to entomb spent fuel for millennia.

top.title: Bank of Korea holds rates as BOJ flags stagflation risk
top.description: South Korea kept its benchmark rate at 2.50% for a seventh straight meeting while Japan's wholesale inflation accelerated amid Middle East energy shock.

top.title: TSMC Q1 revenue jumps 35%, beating forecasts on AI demand
top.description: The world's largest contract chipmaker posted $35.7 billion in quarterly sales, topping estimates as AI chip orders held firm despite Middle East tensions.

top.title: Xi Jinping meets Taiwan's KMT leader in Beijing
top.description: Cheng Li-wun's visit to the Great Hall of the People marks the first meeting between a sitting KMT chair and China's leader in a decade.

top.title: Lawsuit filed after USC freshman loses eye at protest
top.description: An attorney filed a federal tort claim against DHS on behalf of Tucker Collins, 18, who was blinded by a projectile at a March 28 "No Kings" rally in L.A.

top.title: New charter lets Kennedy reshape CDC vaccine panel after court loss
top.description: The revised charter broadens who can serve on the advisory committee and shifts its focus toward vaccine injury claims long promoted by skeptics.

top.title: Judge finds Pentagon violating order on press access
top.description: U.S. District Judge Paul Friedman ruled Thursday that the Defense Department defied his earlier order by imposing new restrictions that still block meaningful reporter access.

top.title: OpenAI pitches compute edge over Anthropic as revenue gap widens
top.description: Bloomberg reports OpenAI told investors this week that its infrastructure spending gives it a key advantage over Anthropic, which recently surpassed OpenAI's revenue run rate.

top.title: White House warns staff against prediction market bets amid Iran trading scrutiny
top.description: The directive followed suspicious trading surges that repeatedly preceded Trump's announcements on the Iran conflict, triggering bipartisan legislative action.

top.title: Cuba's Diaz-Canel vows not to resign in historic NBC interview
top.description: The Cuban president told "Meet the Press" that "resigning is not part of our vocabulary," becoming the first Cuban leader on U.S. TV in decades.

top.title: House GOP blocks Democrats' Iran war powers vote during recess
top.description: Rep. Chris Smith gaveled out of a pro forma session Thursday before Democrats could seek unanimous consent on a resolution to end the Iran conflict.

top.title: White House confirms Cuba talks continuing 'at the highest level'
top.description: Press secretary Karoline Leavitt said discussions are ongoing but announced no policy changes, as Díaz-Canel calls talks "difficult."

top.title: Small AI startups win Pentagon contracts after Anthropic split
top.description: Defense-focused firms like Smack Technologies and EdgeRunner AI report surging military interest after the Pentagon designated Anthropic a supply-chain risk.

top.title: US fertility rate fell to record low in 2025, CDC reports
top.description: The lifetime fertility rate dropped to 1.57 births per woman, well below the 2.1 replacement level, despite pro-natalist policy efforts.

top.title: Trump says he's 'very optimistic' on Iran deal amid Lebanon crisis
top.description: VP Vance is set to lead U.S. talks with Iran in Islamabad on Saturday as Israeli strikes on Lebanon strain a days-old ceasefire.

top.title: Software stocks slide as Anthropic's Mythos AI model stokes disruption fears
top.description: Palantir fell sharply and the broader software sector declined after the company withheld wide release of a model that found thousands of zero-day flaws.

top.title: DOJ opens antitrust investigation into NFL over streaming practices over streaming push
top.description: The probe examines whether the league's shift of games to streaming platforms harms consumers and violates its broadcast exemption.

top.title: PCE inflation holds at 2.8% in February as jobless claims jump
top.description: The Fed's preferred inflation gauge stayed unchanged in February while weekly unemployment filings rose more than expected, signaling a mixed economic outlook.

top.title: Earth grew 16% brighter at night since 2014, study finds
top.description: Daily satellite imagery reveals the planet's artificial lighting is far more volatile than scientists assumed, with regional brightening and dimming driven by development, conflict, and policy.

top.title: OpenAI pauses Stargate UK data center over energy costs
top.description: The decision to shelve the sovereign AI infrastructure project is a setback for Prime Minister Starmer's national AI strategy.

top.title: Lobbyists with Trump Jr. ties helped Chinese firm win national security review
top.description: A firm run by Donald Trump Jr.'s hunting companion helped Grand Pharmaceutical defeat a U.S. national security review of its investment in a medical-device startup.

top.title: Iran war doubles Russia's oil tax revenue to $9 bn in April
top.description: Reuters calculations show the mineral extraction tax windfall follows months of declining energy revenues and a record budget deficit.

top.title: UK forces Russian submarines to retreat from undersea cables
top.description: Britain and Norway tracked three Russian submarines near critical underwater infrastructure for over a month, forcing all vessels to withdraw.

top.title: IUCN lists emperor penguins as endangered over sea ice loss
top.description: The Antarctic fur seal and southern elephant seal were also reclassified as climate change reshapes polar ecosystems.

top.title: New model suggests dark matter has two particle states
top.description: Fermilab physicists propose that dark matter consists of two distinct particles, potentially explaining a longstanding gamma-ray puzzle.

top.title: Study: 2.5 million Americans lost food aid after GOP megabill
top.description: The Center on Budget and Policy Priorities found SNAP enrollment fell sharply in the six months after the One Big Beautiful Bill Act took effect.

top.title: ArcelorMittal donated foreign steel for Trump's ballroom, then got tariff relief
top.description: The Luxembourg-based steelmaker supplied $37 million in steel for the White House project while the administration cut tariffs on its Canadian exports.

top.title: UMass physicists link record-energy neutrino to exploding black hole
top.description: A "dark charge" model proposes primordial black holes from the early universe could explain a 2023 particle detection that defied all known physics.

top.title: North Korea tests cluster warhead on ballistic missile in military trial
top.description: Pyongyang conducted three days of weapons trials, including an electromagnetic weapon system and carbon fiber decoy bombs, drawing condemnation from Seoul.

top.title: Trump asks New York's top court to toss civil fraud finding
top.description: The filing cites former attorney Michael Cohen's claims that he was "pressured and coerced" by prosecutors into testifying against Trump.

top.title: Former Army employee indicted for leaking military secrets to journalist
top.description: Courtney Williams, 40, allegedly shared classified information used in the bestselling book "The Fort Bragg Cartel."

top.title: Anti-war protests sweep US cities despite Iran ceasefire
top.description: Demonstrators in dozens of cities demand a permanent end to the conflict, calling the two-week truce insufficient.

top.title: Trump eyes Fed adviser Christopher Phelan as chief economist
top.description: The Minneapolis Fed adviser and University of Minnesota professor would fill a post vacant since February amid rising gas prices.

top.title: Appeals court upholds Pentagon blacklisting of Anthropic
top.description: The D.C. Circuit ruled the military's need to secure AI technology during conflict outweighs financial harm to the company.

top.title: Russian warship escorts sanctioned tankers through English Channel
top.description: The frigate Admiral Grigorovich sailed alongside two oil tankers off England's south coast, defying the UK's threat to seize shadow fleet vessels.

top.title: Minnesota districts ask judge to block ICE enforcement near schools
top.description: Two school districts and the state's teachers union urged a federal judge Wednesday to restore restrictions rescinded by the Trump administration.

top.title: 'Ketamine Queen' sentenced to 15 years in Matthew Perry's death
top.description: Jasveen Sangha, who sold the fatal dose of ketamine to the "Friends" star, apologized for her "hand in this tragic outcome" before sentencing.

top.title: UAE demands Iran pay reparations after 40 days of attacks
top.description: Abu Dhabi seeks clarity on the U.S.-Iran ceasefire terms while declaring victory after intercepting thousands of missiles and drones.

top.title: Attorney says man shot by ICE was acquitted, had no warrant
top.description: The lawyer for Carlos Ivan Mendoza Hernandez presented Salvadoran court documents contradicting DHS claims that his client was a wanted gang member.

top.title: Iran’s parliament speaker says cease-fire terms already violated
top.description: Iran's parliament speaker says three clauses of Tehran's 10-point framework have been breached, as Israeli strikes kill at least 254 in Lebanon.

top.title: Fed minutes reveal growing caution over Iran war, inflation
top.description: Wednesday's release showed policymakers increasingly wary of cutting rates as oil prices and geopolitical risks cloud the economic outlook.

top.title: Zeldin tells climate skeptics to 'celebrate' endangerment finding repeal
top.description: The EPA chief defended his agency's rollback of the foundational climate rule at a Heartland Institute conference in Washington on Wednesday.

top.title: Trump weighs relocating troops to punish NATO allies over Iran war
top.description: The plan would move U.S. forces away from countries deemed uncooperative during the conflict and into more supportive member states.

top.title: Sharif's 'draft' post gaffe raises questions about Pakistan's mediator role
top.description: PM Shehbaz Sharif accidentally posted a "Draft" label on his ceasefire appeal, fueling speculation the message was scripted by Washington.

top.title: Iran faces economic collapse after 40 days of war
top.description: A two-week ceasefire halted U.S.-Israeli bombardment, but Reuters reports Iran is "near the brink of economic collapse" with GDP expected to contract sharply.

top.title: Pentagon says US ready to resume Iran combat if ceasefire falters
top.description: Defense Secretary Hegseth and Gen. Caine declared military victory but warned forces will "restart at a moment's notice" as Iranian strikes test the truce.

top.title: Meta unveils Muse Spark, its first AI model from superintelligence team
top.description: The closed-source model now powers Meta AI in the U.S., with plans to expand across Instagram, WhatsApp, Facebook, and smart glasses.

top.title: Jeff Shell fired as Paramount president after leak allegations
top.description: Shell's ouster follows a $150 million lawsuit accusing him of leaking confidential deal information to a high-stakes gambler.

top.title: Israel learned of Iran cease-fire deal at last minute
top.description: Netanyahu was informed by Trump only moments before the two-week truce was announced, and Israel was not part of the negotiations, according to the Journal.

top.title: DOJ says fired AG Bondi won't appear for Epstein deposition
top.description: The department argues her subpoena was tied to a role she no longer holds, but the Oversight Committee says it will pursue her testimony.

top.title: IRGC takes de facto control of Iran as supreme leader reportedly incapacitated
top.description: Multiple intelligence sources indicate Mojtaba Khamenei is unconscious in Qom, unable to govern, as a military council runs the country.

top.title: Russia pushes three lunar missions to 2032-2036
top.description: The delays were announced the same day NASA's Artemis II crew completed a historic moon flyby, the first crewed lunar flight since 1972.

top.title: NYT names Adam Back as likely Bitcoin creator Satoshi Nakamoto
top.description: A yearlong investigation built a circumstantial case using writing analysis and timeline gaps, but Back denied being Satoshi more than six times.

top.title: Hegseth declares US victory as Iran ceasefire takes effect
top.description: Trump agreed to a two-week ceasefire Tuesday night after a 38-day air campaign, contingent on Iran reopening the Strait of Hormuz.

top.title: SK Hynix jumps 15% as Samsung's record forecast lifts chip stocks
top.description: Samsung's preliminary Q1 operating profit of 57.2 trillion won, an eightfold annual increase, fueled a broader rally in Korean chipmakers.

top.title: IATA chief warns jet fuel recovery will take months despite ceasefire
top.description: Willie Walsh said Strait of Hormuz disruptions damaged Middle East refining capacity, meaning supply cannot quickly rebound even after a U.S.-Iran ceasefire.

top.title: Iran ceasefire reinforces view US emerged weakened, Bloomberg reports
top.description: Allies and adversaries see the 40-day campaign as a strategic setback that bolstered China and Russia while draining American military and diplomatic capital.

top.title: U.S. rebuffs Ford's request for aluminum tariff relief
top.description: The denial comes as fires at a key Novelis plant have disrupted 40% of U.S. auto aluminum supply since September 2025, according to The Wall Street Journal.

top.title: Amsterdam researchers build metamaterials that learn to change shape
top.description: Worm-like synthetic materials can learn, forget, and relearn new shapes without a central processor, mimicking living systems.

top.title: China helped push Iran to accept ceasefire, AP reports
top.description: Beijing urged Tehran to agree to the two-week truce brokered by Pakistan, as peace talks are set to begin Friday in Islamabad.

top.title: Judge preserves abortion pill mail access, warns FDA review can't wait
top.description: A Louisiana federal judge declined to block mifepristone prescriptions by mail but signaled the state could eventually prevail.

top.title: Fuller wins Georgia runoff to replace Greene by narrow margin
top.description: Trump-backed Republican Clay Fuller defeated Democrat Shawn Harris by roughly 4 points in one of the most conservative districts in the country.

top.title: US, Israel strike Iran's largest aluminum plant
top.description: Strikes on IRALCO in Arak sparked a major fire as Trump warned "a whole civilization will die tonight" if Iran doesn't reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

top.title: Appeals panel signals 'Alligator Alcatraz' can stay open
top.description: Judges on the 11th Circuit questioned whether the Florida ICE facility qualifies as a federal action requiring environmental review.

top.title: CIA used secret 'Ghost Murmur' tool to find airman in Iran
top.description: The never-before-deployed technology uses quantum magnetometry to detect a human heartbeat, pinpointing the injured crew member hiding in a mountain crevice.

top.title: Kuwait orders residents indoors as Trump's Iran deadline looms
top.description: Kuwait's Interior Ministry urged citizens and residents to stay home overnight as Trump's 8 p.m. ET deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz approaches.

top.title: Nearly 1,000 migrants died in Mediterranean in 2026 so far, IOM says
top.description: The UN migration agency called it one of the deadliest starts to a year since it began tracking data in 2014, with deaths up over 150% in the central Mediterranean.

top.title: ICE arrested 800+ people using TSA traveler data
top.description: Internal ICE records reviewed by Reuters reveal the scope of airport-based immigration enforcement far exceeds prior public estimates.

top.title: Acting AG Blanche says Trump has 'right' to influence DOJ probes
top.description: In his first press conference, Blanche also announced a new National Fraud Enforcement Division and denied targeting Trump's political enemies.

top.title: Vance says he's 'here to help' Orban win Hungary election
top.description: The vice president accused the EU and Ukrainian intelligence of meddling in Hungary's April 12 vote, offering no evidence for either claim.

top.title: UK bars Ye from entering country, Wireless Festival canceled
top.description: The Home Office denied the rapper's travel authorization over antisemitism concerns, and organizers scrapped the three-day London event entirely.

top.title: UK, Canada push back on Trump's Iran threats as 8 p.m. deadline nears
top.description: Downing Street signaled it won't allow U.S. use of British bases for infrastructure strikes, while Carney urged respect for international law.

top.title: Turkish drillship arrives in Somali waters for historic first offshore oil drilling
top.description: The Çağrı Bey will target the Curad-1 well, roughly 370 km off the coast, in a region estimated to hold up to 40 billion barrels of oil and gas.

top.title: Tehran's Rafi-Nia Synagogue destroyed in US-Israeli strikes
top.description: Iran's Jewish community condemned the destruction after overnight airstrikes leveled the historic synagogue, burying Torah scrolls under rubble.

top.title: US strikes Kharg Island again as war with Iran intensifies
top.description: Trump warned "a whole civilization will die tonight" if Iran doesn't agree to a deal, as airstrikes pounded Tehran and missiles hit Saudi Arabia.

top.title: Wall Street firm sends analyst into Strait of Hormuz by speedboat
top.description: Citrini Research dispatched a field analyst with $15,000 in cash and hidden recording gear to observe Iranian shipping controls firsthand.

top.title: IEA chief says Middle East war will accelerate renewables shift
top.description: Fatih Birol told Le Figaro the energy crisis is worse than those of 1973, 1979 and 2022 combined but will transform global energy geopolitics.

top.title: Trump lawyers invoke immunity ruling to claim sweeping executive power
top.description: An ABC News review found the administration cited the 2024 decision at least 21 times in emergency appeals to expand presidential authority.

top.title: Leaked transcript shows Orbán offered to serve Putin
top.description: Bloomberg published a Hungarian government transcript of an October call in which Orbán told Putin, "I am at your service."

top.title: Iran war energy shock accelerates global return to nuclear power
top.description: Countries across Asia are racing to restart reactors and build new plants as the Middle East conflict disrupts roughly one-fifth of global LNG supply.

top.title: Deere agrees to $99M settlement and digital tools in right-to-repair suit
top.description: The proposed deal also commits the company to providing farmers digital repair tools for a decade. A separate FTC lawsuit remains active.

top.title: SpaceX details record IPO plans, eyes June roadshow
top.description: CFO Bret Johnsen told bankers Monday that retail investors will get "a bigger part than any IPO in history," with a June 11 event for 1,500 individual investors.

top.title: Federal judge upholds Dugan's ICE obstruction conviction
top.description: U.S. District Judge Lynn Adelman rejected the former Milwaukee County judge's bid for acquittal or a new trial, clearing the way for sentencing and a 7th Circuit appeal.

top.title: Artemis II crew witnesses solar eclipse after shattering distance record
top.description: The four astronauts saw a 53-minute total solar eclipse from beyond the Moon, capping a day of record-breaking firsts during humanity's return to lunar orbit.

top.title: Iran-launched drone kills couple in Erbil village
top.description: The Peshmerga ministry warned it may abandon restraint if Baghdad fails to act against militias behind the escalating aerial campaign.

top.title: Iran launches fresh missile barrage at southern Israel
top.description: Coordinated attacks from Iran, Yemen, and Hezbollah mark a new multi-front escalation as the war enters its sixth week.

top.title: Israel orders evacuation of 41 towns in southern Lebanon
top.description: The Monday evening directive expands the buffer zone to the Zahrani River as airstrikes intensify across Lebanon, killing dozens over the weekend.

top.title: Iranian drone strike wounds 15 US troops at Kuwait air base
top.description: CBS News reports most of the injured service members at Ali Al Salem Air Base have returned to duty, as the US casualty toll in the Iran war rises to 373 wounded.

top.title: USS Murtha departs San Diego to recover Artemis II crew
top.description: The Navy recovery ship set sail Monday as the four astronauts performed their lunar flyby, with splashdown set for Friday.

top.title: Saudi Arabia intercepts missiles as debris falls near energy sites
top.description: Up to 11 ballistic missiles were fired at the kingdom's oil-rich Eastern Province, with damage assessment ongoing at undisclosed energy facilities.

top.title: Four IDF combat soldiers arrested for suspected Iran espionage
top.description: The arrests mark one of the most serious spy cases since the war began, with two other Iran-linked espionage affairs revealed the same day.

top.title: Minneapolis releases video contradicting ICE's account of January shooting
top.description: Footage shows a 12-second scuffle, not the three-minute assault with a shovel and broom that officers described under oath.

top.title: Rep. Ansari moves to impeach Hegseth over Iran war
top.description: The Arizona Democrat, the only Iranian American in Congress, accused the defense secretary of war crimes and reckless endangerment of U.S. troops.

top.title: UN issues urgent call for aid as Cuba crisis worsens
top.description: Blackouts exceeding 18 hours a day affect millions as the UN unveils an updated plan to support two million people across eight provinces.

top.title: Israeli strikes kill at least 10 near Gaza school
top.description: A WHO driver was also killed in a separate attack, prompting the suspension of all medical evacuations from Gaza via Rafah.

top.title: Lutnick set for May 6 House interview on Epstein ties
top.description: The commerce secretary's voluntary transcribed interview before the Oversight Committee follows months of bipartisan pressure over his connections to the late sex offender.

top.title: OpenAI urges state AGs to probe Musk ahead of April trial
top.description: The company accused Musk of waging a destabilization campaign, including allegedly coordinating with Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg.

top.title: Education Dept. terminates agreements protecting trans students at six schools
top.description: The department ended Title IX settlements with five school districts and a college that required protections for transgender students from discrimination.

top.title: White House proposes cutting 9,400 TSA jobs, $1.5B from budget
top.description: The plan would shrink the post-9/11 agency's workforce by roughly 16% and reduce its $7.8 billion budget by about 20%, according to budget documents.

top.title: US-Israeli strikes destroy AI data center at Tehran university
top.description: The predawn attack reduced Sharif University of Technology's ICT building to rubble, also damaging labs, a mosque, and faculty buildings.

top.title: Appeals court bars New Jersey from regulating Kalshi
top.description: The Third Circuit ruled 2-1 that federal commodities law preempts state gambling enforcement, marking Kalshi's first appellate victory.

top.title: Trump threatens to jail journalist over Iran F-15 rescue leak
top.description: The president claims media reporting on the downed jet tipped off Iran and endangered the wounded airman's rescue behind enemy lines.

top.title: Study of 1,700 languages finds shared grammar rules
top.description: About one-third of long-proposed "linguistic universals" hold up under modern statistical testing, pointing to deep cognitive forces shaping all human languages.

top.title: Supreme Court vacates Bannon's contempt-of-Congress conviction
top.description: The justices returned the case to a lower court where prosecutors have already moved to dismiss the indictment tied to Bannon's Jan. 6 subpoena defiance.
